brand_dna.category: A Los Angeles general store curating cozy, functional homeware, kitchen, and gift objects rooted in Taiwanese design.
brand_dna.core_belief: 惜物 - to cherish what you own. Care for and cherish material things out of respect for the craft, the maker, and the resources behind every object. Buy less, choose well, use it for a long time.
proof_points: Rooted in Taiwanese design; many pieces Made in Taiwan (e.g. Taiwanese Beer Cup)
Made by hand by makers who care about craft and material
Handwoven in the Philippines using traditional weaving techniques (Shopping Bag)
Every order ships with tracking, 5-7 days domestic
brand_dna.positioning: Slow-living, curated and intentional - the opposite of mindless consumption, priced accessibly rather than luxury.

voice_pillars.name: Calm and Cozy
voice_pillars.desc: Warm, unguarded language that centers ease and comfort in everyday living.
voice_pillars.quote: that easy, unguarded feeling of being fully at ease

voice_pillars.name: Craft-Reverent
voice_pillars.desc: Speaks with respect for makers, materials, and the intention behind each object.
voice_pillars.quote: made by makers who care about craft and material

voice_pillars.name: Intentional Minimalism
voice_pillars.desc: Frames buying as a deliberate choice against waste and excess.
voice_pillars.quote: BUY LESS. CHOOSE WELL. USE IT FOR A LONG TIME.

voice_pillars.name: Playful Culture
voice_pillars.desc: Leans into Taiwanese ritual and celebration with lively, communal warmth.
voice_pillars.quote: Small size encourages casual sipping and frequent toasts

phrases: cozy, functional objects for everyday living
objects that feel good to hold, easy to live with, and worth keeping
the joy of gathering rather than the pressure of heavy drinking
shared laughter and spontaneous "Gan Bei!" cheers
expands to hold more than you'd expect
using the same cup every morning until it feels like yours

icp: Design-conscious home lovers who value slow living, craft, and cultural story, and buy objects meant to be kept and used for years rather than replaced.

personas.name: The Slow-Living Curator
personas.description: A design-minded homemaker who wants fewer, better objects that age beautifully; moved by craft, material, and the 惜物 philosophy.

personas.name: The Thoughtful Gifter
personas.description: A shopper hunting a meaningful, well-made present under $50 or $100; moved by story, handmade origin, and gift-ready charm.

personas.name: The Taiwan-Rooted Nostalgic
personas.description: Someone drawn to Taiwanese food culture and design; moved by authentic rituals like the 143ml beer cup and 'Gan Bei' toasts.

products_ladder.title: Stemless White Wine Glass 12oz  | TG x Naoto Fukasawa

offers: Every order ships with tracking, 5-7 days domestic
Gift collections: Gift under $50 and Gift under $100, plus Bundles
Returns for Store Credit only (unless defective), within 12 days of delivery, item unused with packaging intact

allowed.claim: Handwoven in the Philippines using traditional weaving techniques
143ml / 4.8oz beer cup, a staple of Taiwanese food culture
Every order ships with tracking, 5-7 days domestic
Made by hand by makers who care about craft and material

banned.claim: Any 'detox', 'boosts immunity', or wellness cure claim tied to the Meditation Shirt or tea ware
banned.why: Unqualified health/immunity claims are banned for this category; product is apparel/homeware, not a health product.

banned.claim: Nutrient-content claims like 'low sugar' or 'high protein' on any consumable sold via tea tastings or food items
banned.why: Nutrient-content claims must match an actual label; none is provided here.

banned.claim: 'Natural' or 'clean' as a headline claim for products or materials
banned.why: 'Natural' and 'clean' are vague and risky as headline claims per category compliance.

banned.claim: Disease-treatment or 'stress-relieving' therapeutic claims for the Meditation Shirt
banned.why: Disease-treatment claims are banned; the shirt is a design piece inspired by a personal story, not a medical device.

banned.claim: 'Eco-friendly' or 'sustainable' as an unqualified blanket claim
banned.why: FTC Green Guides - unqualified sustainability claims require substantiation not present on the site.
